name: Accounting & Bookkeeping
description: Understand financial records, statements, and the language of business

concepts.name: What Is Accounting?
concepts.description: The purpose of accounting: tracking economic activity so decisions can be made with reliable information.

concepts.name: The Accounting Equation
concepts.description: Assets = Liabilities + Equity. Every transaction keeps this equation balanced.

concepts.name: Debits and Credits
concepts.description: The double-entry system: every transaction has equal debits and credits across accounts.

concepts.name: Chart of Accounts
concepts.description: Organizing accounts into categories: assets, liabilities, equity, revenue, and expenses.

concepts.name: Journal Entries
concepts.description: Recording transactions in chronological order with debits and credits.

concepts.name: The General Ledger
concepts.description: Posting journal entries to individual account ledgers and maintaining running balances.

concepts.name: Trial Balance
concepts.description: Summing all account balances to verify that total debits equal total credits.

concepts.name: The Income Statement
concepts.description: Revenue minus expenses equals net income. Measures profitability over a period.

concepts.name: The Balance Sheet
concepts.description: A snapshot of assets, liabilities, and equity at a point in time.

concepts.name: Cash Flow Statement
concepts.description: Tracking cash in and out across operating, investing, and financing activities.

concepts.name: Accrual vs Cash Basis
concepts.description: Recognizing revenue when earned vs when cash is received, and why accrual is the standard.

concepts.name: Adjusting Entries
concepts.description: End-of-period adjustments for prepaid expenses, unearned revenue, depreciation, and accruals.
